context.story_engine: A high school teacher builds a drug empire, testing how far he'll go for family and control

plotlines.name: Walt: Empire
plotlines.goal: build a massive drug operation to secure his family's financial future
plotlines.obstacle: dangerous partners like Tuco, distribution problems, professional competition

plotlines.name: Walt: Family
plotlines.goal: maintain his marriage and family while hiding his criminal transformation
plotlines.obstacle: Skyler's growing suspicions, his own personality changes, mounting lies
plotlines.stakes: family breakdown, exposure of criminal activities

plotlines.name: Jesse: Survival
plotlines.goal: survive and profit in the drug trade while managing his crew
plotlines.obstacle: violent competitors, unreliable dealers, addiction, loss of friends

plotlines.name: Hank: Investigation
plotlines.goal: capture Heisenberg and shut down the blue meth operation
plotlines.obstacle: lack of direct evidence, Heisenberg's careful methods, personal trauma from Tuco shooting
plotlines.stakes: dangerous drugs flooding the streets, career reputation

plotlines.name: Jesse: Love
plotlines.goal: build a relationship with Jane and escape his criminal life
plotlines.obstacle: his drug addiction, Jane's relapse, Walt's interference
plotlines.stakes: loss of love, deeper addiction, death

events.event: Flash-forward shows hazmat suits collecting evidence at White residence with pink teddy bear in pool
Walt and Jesse are held at gunpoint by Tuco after witnessing No-Doze's brutal murder
Walt and Jesse leave Tuco's compound recognizing he's too dangerous to work with or abandon
Jesse urges Walt to disappear but Walt insists they can't run because Tuco knows Jesse's identity
Walt proposes poisoning Tuco with ricin as their solution
Walt synthesizes ricin from castor beans in Jesse's kitchen
Walt conceals ricin inside a bag of meth for their next exchange with Tuco
Skyler confronts Walt about his erratic absences and odd behavior, demanding honesty
Walt deflects Skyler's concerns with vague reassurances
Hank and Gomez identify Tuco as prime suspect in blue meth distribution
DEA escalates surveillance on Tuco based on intelligence about his violence and large purchases
Walt and Jesse prepare for meeting with Tuco, hiding ricin-laced meth in separate bag
Tuco reveals Gonzo died trying to move No-Doze's body when cars collapsed on him
Tuco blames Walt and Jesse for bringing bad luck and forces them into his car at gunpoint
Tuco announces he's taking them to Mexico to cook for cartel and drives away with them as hostages

episodes.theme: the illusion of control and escalating consequences

interactions.description: all storylines involve characters losing control despite their attempts to manage situations - Walt can't control Tuco, Skyler can't get truth from Walt, Hank closes in unknowingly on his brother-in-law
the audience knows Walt is Heisenberg while Skyler and Hank remain unaware, creating tension in their scenes
flash-forward device frames the episode with future consequences

patches.reason: flash-forward sequence doesn't advance any character's current goal but establishes future consequences

events.event: Tuco drives Walt and Jesse to a remote desert shack where his uncle Hector waits in a wheelchair
Tuco forces Walt to cook meth in the shack's crude kitchen while Walt secretly plots to use ricin
Jesse prepares a burrito and stirs ricin-laced meth into it for Tuco
Hector rings his bell and knocks the poisoned plate off the table before Tuco can eat
Tuco beats Jesse severely and drags him outside after the failed poisoning
Skyler calls Jesse's phone repeatedly when Walt doesn't come home, then contacts Hank in panic
Hank discovers Jesse's Monte Carlo with CAPNCOOK plates at Tuco's compound and traces the registration
Walt tells Tuco that Jesse is useless and suggests letting him go, but Tuco refuses
Jesse finds a gun hidden under the porch while bloodied from his beating
Jesse shoots Tuco in the abdomen and Tuco fires back, then Walt strikes Tuco with a rock
Hank arrives at the shack and exchanges gunfire with wounded Tuco in the desert
Hank kills Tuco with a shot to the head
Walt and Jesse flee on foot into the desert before Hank can discover them
Walt and Jesse separate in the desert, each planning alibis for their disappearance

interactions.description: all three storylines involve life-or-death stakes and the lengths people go to survive
Hank unknowingly saves Walt and Jesse by killing Tuco, while Skyler's panic inadvertently leads Hank to the scene
all storylines physically converge at the desert shack for the climactic shootout

events.event: Walt strips naked in a convenience store, feigning a fugue state caused by his cancer
Walt is taken to the hospital and maintains the fugue story to doctors and Skyler
Skyler rushes to the hospital, relieved but deeply confused and doubtful
Doctors run tests and find no neurological cause but acknowledge cancer stress could trigger dissociation
Hank is placed on administrative leave pending review of the Tuco shooting
DEA treats Hank as a hero, but he privately struggles with having killed a man
Hank visits Walt in the hospital and doesn't question the fugue story
Jesse checks into a motel and calls Skinny Pete for a ride back to town
Jesse discovers DEA searched his house and impounded his car
Jesse calls Walt furiously demanding to know what to do
Walt coldly instructs Jesse to stay quiet and say nothing to anyone
Walt assures Jesse the only link was the car with no evidence of their operation
DEA interviews Hector Salamanca about Tuco's death and operations
Hector refuses to cooperate or identify Walt and Jesse out of his code against informing
Walt is released from hospital and returns home to family gathering
Walt sits through dinner performing normalcy while calculating his next move
Walt keeps the ricin vial concealed in his jacket pocket

interactions.description: all storylines explore different forms of psychological burden — Walt's lies, Hank's guilt, the weight of criminal life
the audience knows Walt's fugue state is fake while Skyler and Hank believe it, and that Hector's silence protects Walt
Hank's emotional state from killing Tuco affects his ability to pursue the investigation

events.event: Jesse discovers his parents have changed the locks on his aunt's house
Jesse's parents inform him he's no longer welcome due to the DEA investigation
Jesse pleads with his parents but his father stands firm
Jesse packs and leaves, beginning his homelessness spiral
Jesse crashes at Skinny Pete's place briefly
Jesse moves through increasingly squalid locations, eventually sleeping in the RV
Jesse retrieves the RV by paying the towing fee with his remaining cash
Walt tries to reestablish normal life but finds domestic environment suffocating
Skyler rebuffs Walt's intimacy attempts, sensing dishonesty in his fugue story
Skyler begins investigating Walt by checking phone records and credit card statements
Walt returns to teaching where colleagues treat him with gentle sympathy
Walt contacts Jesse to discuss resuming meth operation but Jesse refuses
Jesse is robbed of his remaining cash at a shelter house
Jesse visits his parents' home but his mother calls police and he flees
Walt discovers his medical bills are mounting and insurance is inadequate
Skyler suggests accepting financial help from Elliott and Gretchen
Walt bristles at accepting help from Gray Matter but doesn't refuse
Jesse calls Walt and agrees to restart meth business as equal partners

interactions.description: all three storylines show characters forced into unwanted compromises by desperate circumstances
Skyler investigates Walt's lies while the audience knows the truth about his criminal activities
Jesse's homelessness crisis forces him back into partnership with Walt

events.event: Walt and Jesse resume cooking meth in the RV, producing a large quantity of blue product
Jesse proposes using Skinny Pete, Badger, and Combo as individual dealers
Walt is skeptical of Jesse's street-level distribution plan but agrees
Jesse distributes meth to his three friends with price and deadline
Skinny Pete is robbed at knifepoint by addicts who take his product and cash
Skinny Pete returns to Jesse empty-handed and humiliated
Jesse realizes his dealers need protection or will be victimized repeatedly
Walt tells Jesse the distribution problem is his responsibility to handle
Hank experiences a panic attack in the DEA elevator but hides it from colleagues
Marie notices Hank's changed behavior and urges him to see a counselor
Hank refuses counseling, insisting he is fine
Skyler arranges dinner with Elliott and Gretchen to discuss paying for Walt's treatment
Walt attends dinner seething with resentment at accepting charity from the Schwartzes
Elliott offers to cover all medical expenses and Walt accepts in the moment
Walt later tells Skyler he will not take the Schwartzes' money
Jesse buys a gun and practices intimidating persona in bathroom mirror
Walt tells Jesse there are two types: those who get robbed and those who rob
Jesse resolves to control his territory through fear rather than trust

interactions.description: all storylines explore different costs of maintaining control — Walt's pride costs him help, Jesse's territory costs him innocence, Hank's facade costs him healing
Skyler believes Walt needs the Schwartzes' help while the audience knows he's making money from meth
Walt's business philosophy directly influences Jesse's transformation into an enforcer

events.event: Jesse and Skinny Pete track down the meth addicts who robbed Skinny Pete
Jesse goes to Spooge's house alone with his gun to confront the thieves
Jesse discovers a neglected five-year-old boy living in squalor
Jesse waits as Spooge attempts to crack open a stolen ATM
Jesse intermittently cares for the boy, adjusting his blanket and changing the TV
Walt tells Skyler that Elliott and Gretchen are paying for his medical bills
Walt begins aggressive chemotherapy that leaves him nauseous and exhausted
Skyler calls Gretchen to thank her, but Gretchen knows nothing about paying for treatment
Spooge's wife pushes the ATM, crushing Spooge's head and killing him
Jesse calls 911 anonymously to report the child in danger
Jesse leads the boy to safety on the front porch before fleeing
Word spreads that Jesse killed Spooge, enhancing his street reputation

interactions.description: both storylines explore protecting children — Jesse literally saves a boy, Walt claims to protect his family through lies
the audience knows Walt is lying about Elliott and Gretchen paying for treatment
the audience knows Jesse didn't kill Spooge, but his reputation benefits from the misattribution

events.event: A narcocorrido music video depicts Heisenberg's rise and celebrates his blue meth
The DEA watches the Heisenberg video with amusement and concern
Hank adds the Heisenberg case to his active investigations
Jesse expands his distribution network with Combo, Skinny Pete, and Badger working separate corners
Jesse manages the operation with increasing confidence, collecting money and distributing batches
Walt continues chemotherapy despite debilitating side effects and insists on cooking
Walt and Jesse produce another large batch in the RV
Skyler contacts Gretchen directly and they meet for lunch
Gretchen is bewildered by Skyler's gratitude for supposed financial support
Skyler realizes Walt has been lying about who is paying for his treatment
Skyler confronts Walt indirectly with pointed questions about the Schwartzes
Walt deflects with anger and storms out after the argument escalates
Walt confronts Gretchen at her home, furious that she spoke with Skyler
Walt tells Gretchen his personal life is none of her business and severs the relationship
Two rival dealers begin encroaching on Combo's territory on the south side
Combo reports the territorial incursion to Jesse, who tells him to hold his ground

interactions.description: all storylines explore how reputation and success create new vulnerabilities — Walt's Heisenberg legend attracts DEA attention, his lies unravel his marriage, Jesse's crew faces territorial challenges
the audience knows Walt is Heisenberg while Hank investigates the very man he's hunting
Skyler discovers Walt's lies about treatment funding but doesn't know the criminal source

events.event: Badger is arrested by an undercover DEA officer while selling meth on a park bench
Hank and Gomez process Badger's arrest and pressure him to identify his supplier
Badger stalls but does not immediately give up Jesse's name
Jesse panics when he learns of the arrest and tells Walt their operation is exposed
Walt agrees that if Badger talks, the chain leads directly to them
Jesse contacts Saul Goodman, a flamboyant defense attorney from the criminal underworld
Jesse hires Saul to represent Badger
Saul visits Badger in holding and assesses the situation
Saul meets with Jesse and Walt and proposes hiring a decoy to pose as Heisenberg
Saul produces Jimmy In-N-Out, a petty criminal willing to take the fall for a fee
Walt is skeptical but agrees to the plan when Saul demonstrates its legal plausibility
Badger identifies Jimmy In-N-Out as his supplier during the sting operation
The DEA arrests Jimmy In-N-Out, believing they have caught Heisenberg
Hank is suspicious because the suspect does not match his Heisenberg profile
Saul offers his ongoing services as a criminal lawyer to Walt and Jesse
Saul proposes connecting Walt with a professional distributor to replace Jesse's street network
Walt is intrigued by Saul's distributor proposal but remains noncommittal
Skyler discovers Walt's second cell phone when it falls out of his bag
Skyler confronts Walt about the phone, and he lies that it's a work replacement
Skyler clearly does not believe Walt's lie about the phone

interactions.description: all storylines involve characters trying to protect something valuable at increasing cost — Walt protects his operation and family through deception, Jesse protects his crew through criminal lawyers, Skyler tries to protect her marriage by confronting lies
the audience knows Walt is Heisenberg while Hank investigates him and Skyler suspects him without knowing the truth
Badger's arrest forces Walt and Jesse's storylines to merge through their shared need for Saul's services

events.event: Walt receives test results showing his tumor has shrunk significantly and he is in remission
Walt feels unsettling emptiness rather than relief about his remission
Walt calculates his finances and realizes he has far less money saved than expected after expenses
Walt determines he and Jesse need to cook a massive quantity to secure his family's financial future
Walt proposes a marathon four-day cook session in the desert using the RV
Jesse agrees reluctantly, noting they are low on methylamine
Walt purchases all the supplies they can afford and they drive the RV deep into the desert
The cook proceeds efficiently with Walt and Jesse falling into their established routine
Jesse accidentally drains the RV's battery by leaving the key in the ignition to power the radio
Walt and Jesse realize they are stranded in the desert with no cell signal, no water supply, and climbing temperatures
Walt attempts multiple failed solutions including jump-starting the battery and considering siphoning gasoline
Walt and Jesse argue bitterly about whose fault the situation is, with Jesse blaming Walt for choosing such a remote location
Walt collapses from heat, exhaustion, and his weakened post-chemo condition as dehydration sets in
Jesse becomes desperate and nearly gives up as their situation becomes dire
Walt engineers a makeshift battery using coins, galvanized metal, and potassium hydroxide to generate enough current to start the engine
The RV starts and they drive back to Albuquerque with a full batch of meth and their lives barely intact
Walt returns home and tells Skyler nothing of the ordeal
Jesse delivers the product and they now have enough meth to make a significant deal but still no large-scale distributor

interactions.description: both storylines explore how Walt's remission paradoxically drives him deeper into crime rather than back to normalcy
Skyler believes Walt is recovering and their ordeal is ending, while the audience knows his remission is pushing him toward greater criminal ambition

events.event: Walt publicly announces his cancer remission at a family gathering organized by Skyler
Walt appears detached and restless rather than grateful during the celebration
Walt buys Walt Jr. a Dodge Challenger without consulting Skyler
Skyler demands to know where the money came from, Walt brushes her off
Walt drinks heavily at a pool party and forces Walt Jr. to drink tequila
Hank intervenes and Walt challenges him aggressively over the drinking
Hank physically takes the bottle away, Walt stares him down defiantly
Walt tells Jesse they should not rush to find a new distributor
Jesse argues that product sitting in storage is a liability, Walt dismisses the concern
Walt encounters a young man buying meth-making supplies at the hardware store
Walt threatens the young cook to stay out of his territory
Skyler tells Walt she no longer recognizes him, he insists he's the same person
Walt and Skyler grow distant with silence at dinner and separate bedtimes
Walt stares at the ceiling unable to sleep, his appetite for power unchanged by remission

interactions.description: both storylines explore how Walt's true nature emerges when the cancer excuse is removed
the audience knows Walt's reckless behavior stems from his drug empire success, while Skyler attributes it to cancer remission relief

events.event: Combo is shot and killed by a young boy while dealing on rival territory
Jesse blames himself for Combo's death and is devastated by the loss
Jesse shuts down the street-level distribution network and tells Skinny Pete and Badger to stop dealing
Jesse spirals emotionally and begins using meth again, isolating himself
Walt realizes their independent distribution model is fatally flawed
Walt contacts Saul and asks him to arrange the meeting with the professional distributor
Saul warns Walt about the distributor's serious nature but makes the call
Walt waits at Los Pollos Hermanos but no one approaches him
Walt returns the next day and again receives no contact
Walt confronts Gus directly and asks if he is the distributor
Gus denies involvement and politely suggests Walt leave
Walt tells Saul the connection is a dead end
Saul advises patience, explaining Gus evaluates partners over time
Jesse meets Jane Margolis, his new neighbor and landlord's daughter
Jane reveals she's an artist and recovering addict who attends NA meetings
Jesse and Jane begin a tentative friendship while Jesse hides his drug involvement
Skyler discovers Walt has been making calls to an unidentified number
Skyler's trust in Walt continues to erode but she doesn't act yet
Gus investigates Walt's background through his own sources
Gus sends word through Saul that he's willing to meet, but only with Walt

interactions.description: all storylines explore the tension between independence and partnership — Walt needs Gus, Jesse needs to rebuild after going solo, Jesse and Jane both struggle with isolation in recovery
both Jane and Skyler are unaware of Jesse and Walt's true criminal involvement
Combo's death forces both Walt and Jesse to confront the failure of their independent operation

events.event: Walt meets Gus at Los Pollos Hermanos for a formal business conversation
Gus explains he has verified Walt's product as exceptional but has reservations about Jesse as unreliable
Gus offers to buy Walt's entire stockpile for $1.2 million in a one-time deal with one-hour delivery deadline
Walt calls Jesse repeatedly but cannot reach him
Walt finds Jesse in a drugged stupor with Jane, who has relapsed into heroin use
Walt retrieves the meth himself and races to the drop point, barely making the deadline
Gus's men complete the exchange and Walt receives $1.2 million in cash
Walt brings Jesse's money but refuses to hand it over, saying he'll hold it until Jesse is sober
Jesse accuses Walt of treating him like a child
Jane encourages Jesse to demand his money and suggests they use it to start a new life together
Jesse calls Walt and insists on receiving his share immediately, Walt refuses
Jane calls Walt threatening to expose his drug operation if he doesn't hand over Jesse's money
Walt agrees to deliver the cash under Jane's threat
Walt finds Jesse and Jane passed out from heroin use
Walt accidentally rolls Jane onto her back while trying to rouse Jesse
Jane begins to vomit and choke, Walt watches her asphyxiate instead of saving her
Walt leaves the house as Jane's breathing stops

interactions.description: both storylines explore the lengths Walt will go to maintain control — over his business and over Jesse's choices
Jane's threat to expose Walt's operation forces the storylines to merge, culminating in her death
the audience knows Walt could save Jane but chooses not to, while Jesse remains unaware of Walt's role in her death

events.event: Jesse wakes up next to Jane's dead body and calls 911 in hysterics
Paramedics arrive and pronounce Jane dead from accidental overdose
Walt arrives at Jesse's house feigning shock over Jane's death
Walt arranges through Saul to have Jesse admitted to rehab
Mike cleans Jesse's house of drug evidence before police arrive
Donald Margolis is destroyed by Jane's death and returns to work grief-stricken
Walt undergoes surgery while Skyler drives him to the hospital
Walt nearly confesses about his second phone while groggy from anesthesia
Skyler visits Walt after surgery with cold and distant manner
Jesse enters rehab to confront his addiction and grief over Jane
Walt returns home and stares at his bag of cash in the crawl space
Donald fails to issue correction causing midair collision of two planes
Pink teddy bear falls from plane wreckage into White family backyard
Walt watches news coverage of the plane crash that killed 167 people
Skyler tells Walt she wants him to move out

episodes.theme: the devastating consequences of moral compromise

interactions.description: all storylines explore how Walt's choices destroy relationships and lives
the audience knows Walt caused Jane's death and the plane crash, but Skyler and Jesse don't
the plane crash serves as a metaphorical culmination of Walt's destructive impact

patches.reason: Donald's grief and the plane crash represent a major consequence storyline that doesn't fit existing categories
